# LLM Gateway

> Lightweight LLM API gateway with multi-provider routing, automatic fallback, and sticky routing.

## What It Does

Sits between your app and multiple LLM providers. Routes requests, retries on failure, sticks to working deployments, converts between OpenAI and Anthropic protocols automatically.

## API (Management)

Base: http://localhost:3456/api

### Providers
- GET /api/providers — list all providers
- POST /api/providers — create provider { name, baseUrl, apiKey, apiType: "openai"|"anthropic" }
- PUT /api/providers/:id — update provider
- DELETE /api/providers/:id — delete provider
- POST /api/providers/:id/fetch-models — auto-discover models from provider

### Models
- GET /api/models — list all models
- POST /api/models — create model { name }
- PUT /api/models/:id — update model
- DELETE /api/models/:id — delete model

### Deployments (model → provider binding)
- GET /api/deployments — list all deployments
- POST /api/deployments — create { modelId, providerId, modelName, order, timeout, maxRetries, enabled }
- PUT /api/deployments/:id — update deployment
- DELETE /api/deployments/:id — delete deployment

### Chains (fallback sequences)
- GET /api/chains — list chains
- POST /api/chains — create { name, mode: "models"|"provider", items: JSON }
- PUT /api/chains/:id — update chain
- DELETE /api/chains/:id — delete chain

### Sticky/Pin
- POST /api/sticky — pin deployment { modelName, deploymentId, ttlMs? }
- DELETE /api/sticky/:model — clear sticky for model/chain
- DELETE /api/sticky — clear all

### Proxy (LLM requests)
- POST /v1/chat/completions — OpenAI-compatible
- POST /v1/messages — Anthropic-compatible

Model name in request maps to a model or chain name in the gateway.

## Typical Setup Flow

1. Create a provider (name, baseUrl, apiKey, apiType)
2. Fetch or manually create models
3. Create deployments linking models to providers
4. Optionally create a chain for fallback routing
5. Point your app at the gateway's /v1/chat/completions or /v1/messages
